turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, we’ve just started our group project. Can we plan the tasks together?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, that sounds like a good idea. Maybe we can first list all the tasks we need to complete.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: We’ll need research, writing, and a presentation.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Agreed. I’m comfortable with research, but I’d prefer less writing if possible.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Well, let’s talk about how to divide the research and writing sections fairly.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: How about we split the research evenly, then each person writes one section based on their research?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: That could work, but I’m worried about the workload balance.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s fair. We could set word limits so everyone has a similar amount.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It sounds like we have different views. Can we find a middle option?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes—maybe two people focus more on research, and two focus more on writing, but everyone contributes a bit to both.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Sounds good! I’m happy to try the presentation, but I’ve never done it before. Any tips?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: No problem. Practising early and using simple slides really helps. I can review your slides with you.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Do you think we need a shared document to manage tasks?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Definitely. Let’s use Google Docs and add a task list with deadlines.
